question:
Unscramble:
TAC
solution: TAC unscrambles to CAT.

question:
Unscramble:
GOD
solution: GOD unscrambles to DOG.

question:
Unscramble:
NUS
solution: NUS unscrambles to SUN.

question:
Unscramble:
KOOB
solution: KOOB unscrambles to BOOK.

question:
Unscramble:
ERTE
hint: It has a trunk, branches and leaves.
solution: ERTE unscrambles to TREE.

question:
Fill the missing letter:
H_USE
solution: The missing letter is O — HOUSE.

question:
Fill the missing letter:
WAT_R
solution: The missing letter is E — WATER.

question:
Complete the proverb:
Honesty is the best ___
hint: An insurance company also sells one.
solution: The proverb is 'Honesty is the best policy.'

question:
Unscramble:
OUSME
hint: A small animal that cats chase.
solution: OUSME unscrambles to MOUSE.

question:
Unscramble:
RTHEA
solution: RTHEA unscrambles to EARTH (HEART and HATER also work).

question:
Complete the proverb:
Better late than ___
solution: The proverb is 'Better late than never.'

question:
What is the longest word?
There's a mile between its first and last letter.
hint: A happy face makes them — plural.
solution: SMILES — there is a MILE between the first S and the last S.

question: What word is spelled wrong in every dictionary?
solution: The word WRONG — it is literally spelled 'wrong' everywhere.

question:
Unscramble:
NELOM
solution: NELOM unscrambles to LEMON (MELON also works).

question:
Complete the proverb:
Practice makes ___
solution: The proverb is 'Practice makes perfect.'

question:
Find the animal hidden in:
"The doctors operate tonight"
hint: A rodent, hiding inside a single word.
solution: opeRATe — the word RAT hides inside 'operate'.

question:
Unscramble:
OWNDIW
solution: OWNDIW unscrambles to WINDOW.

question:
Complete the proverb:
An ___ a day keeps the doctor away
solution: The proverb is 'An apple a day keeps the doctor away.'

question:
Which 5-letter word becomes shorter
when you add two letters to it?
solution: SHORT — add E and R and it becomes SHORTER.

question:
Unscramble:
TIGRE
solution: TIGRE unscrambles to TIGER.

question:
Unscramble:
LCHOSO
solution: LCHOSO unscrambles to SCHOOL.

question:
What begins with T, ends with T,
and has T in it?
hint: You brew a hot drink in it.
solution: TEAPOT — begins with T, ends with T, and holds tea (T) inside.

question:
Complete the proverb:
Actions speak louder than ___
hint: This app is full of them.
solution: The proverb is 'Actions speak louder than words.'

question:
Unscramble:
TRUPICE
hint: You take one with a camera.
solution: TRUPICE unscrambles to PICTURE.

question:
Forward I am heavy,
backward I am not.
What am I?
hint: A very heavy unit of weight.
solution: TON — read backward it spells NOT.

question:
Which one is the odd one out?
APPLE · BANANA · CARROT · MANGO
hint: Three are fruits; one grows underground.
solution: CARROT is a vegetable — the rest are fruits.

question:
Complete the proverb:
Don't count your ___ before they hatch
solution: The proverb is 'Don't count your chickens before they hatch.'

question:
Unscramble:
DERNGA
solution: DERNGA unscrambles to GARDEN (DANGER, RANGED, GANDER also work).

question:
Which word contains all five vowels
in order (a, e, i, o, u)?
hint: It means treating serious things with humor. Starts with F.
solution: FACETIOUS — f-A-c-E-t-I-O-U-s, vowels in order.

question:
Find the body part hidden in:
"She was rich indeed"
solution: riCH INdeed — CHIN hides across 'rich indeed'.

question:
Unscramble:
MOLPRBE
solution: MOLPRBE unscrambles to PROBLEM.

question:
Name a 4-letter palindrome —
a word that reads the same backward.
solution: NOON — the same forward and backward (DEED, PEEP, TOOT also work).

question:
Complete the proverb:
The early bird catches the ___
solution: The proverb is 'The early bird catches the worm.'

question:
Which one is the odd one out?
RED · BLUE · GREEN · CIRCLE
solution: CIRCLE is a shape — the rest are colours.

question:
Unscramble:
YRTAEBT
solution: YRTAEBT unscrambles to BATTERY.

question:
It has keys but no locks,
space but no room.
You can enter but not go in.
solution: A KEYBOARD — keys, a space bar, and an enter key.

question:
Complete the proverb:
When in Rome, do as the ___ do
solution: The proverb is 'When in Rome, do as the Romans do.'

question:
Unscramble:
NIATNOUM
solution: NIATNOUM unscrambles to MOUNTAIN.

question:
What 7-letter word has
hundreds of letters in it?
hint: It stands outside your house and holds mail.
solution: MAILBOX — it holds hundreds of letters (the mail kind).

question:
Name a 5-letter palindrome
(reads the same both ways).
hint: You are playing one right now.
solution: LEVEL — same forward and backward (CIVIC, RADAR, KAYAK also work).

question:
Unscramble:
ROPSTRANT
hint: Moving people or goods from place to place.
solution: ROPSTRANT unscrambles to TRANSPORT.

question:
Remove my first letter and
I still sound exactly the same.
What word am I?
solution: QUEUE — remove the Q and 'ueue' still sounds like 'cue'.

question:
Complete the proverb:
Every cloud has a silver ___
hint: The inside layer of a jacket has one too.
solution: The proverb is 'Every cloud has a silver lining.'

question:
Find the country hidden in:
"I ran to the store"
hint: A country in the Middle East.
solution: 'I RAN to' — the country IRAN hides at the start.

question:
Unscramble:
CLEEHIV
hint: Cars and buses are types of it.
solution: CLEEHIV unscrambles to VEHICLE.

question:
What has a bed but never sleeps,
and runs but never walks?
solution: A RIVER — it has a riverbed and it runs.

question:
Which one is the odd one out?
CAR · BUS · TRAIN · ROAD
solution: ROAD — the rest are vehicles.

question:
Which word has three consecutive
double letters?
hint: A person who keeps financial records.
solution: BOOKKEEPER — b-OO-KK-EE-per.

question:
Unscramble:
YRARBIL
hint: A quiet place full of books.
solution: YRARBIL unscrambles to LIBRARY.

question:
The more you take,
the more you leave behind.
What are they?
hint: You make them when you walk.
solution: FOOTSTEPS — every step you take leaves one behind.

question:
Complete the proverb:
Where there's a will, there's a ___
solution: The proverb is 'Where there's a will, there's a way.'

question:
Unscramble:
TROCMPUE
hint: You might be holding a small one right now.
solution: TROCMPUE unscrambles to COMPUTER.

question:
Which 8-letter word remains a word
each time you remove a letter,
all the way down to one?
hint: The opposite of finishing. Begins with S.
solution: STARTING → STARING → STRING → STING → SING → SIN → IN → I.

question:
Find the insect hidden in:
"The vibe eventually improved"
solution: viBE Eventually — BEE hides across the two words.

question:
Unscramble:
TOICANER
hint: The act of making something new.
solution: TOICANER unscrambles to CREATION (REACTION also works).

question:
I am an odd number.
Take away a letter and I become even.
What number am I?
solution: SEVEN — remove the S and it becomes EVEN.

question:
Which one is the odd one out?
JANUARY · MONDAY · MARCH · AUGUST
solution: MONDAY is a day of the week — the rest are months.

question:
Name a word that uses all five vowels
exactly once.
solution: SEQUOIA — a, e, i, o, u each appear exactly once.

question:
Unscramble:
DRAONIIYTC
hint: A book full of word meanings.
solution: DRAONIIYTC unscrambles to DICTIONARY.

question:
What word, written in capitals,
reads the same upside down?
hint: Something you do in a pool.
solution: SWIMS — rotate it 180° and it still reads SWIMS.

question:
Unscramble:
TNEMNORIVNE
solution: TNEMNORIVNE unscrambles to ENVIRONMENT.

question:
Remove my first letter, I stay the same.
Remove my last letter, unchanged too.
Even remove my middle — still the same.
What am I?
solution: EMPTY — take anything from nothing and it is still empty.

question:
Complete the proverb:
A ___ in time saves nine
hint: Made with a needle and thread.
solution: The proverb is 'A stitch in time saves nine.'

question:
Find the fruit hidden across:
"He came long ago"
solution: caME LONg — MELON hides across 'came long'.

question:
Unscramble:
YHPOSOLIHP
hint: The study of wisdom and existence.
solution: YHPOSOLIHP unscrambles to PHILOSOPHY.

question: What word begins and ends with "UND"?
solution: UNDERGROUND — UND-ergro-UND.

question:
Which one is the odd one out?
OCEAN · RIVER · LAKE · DESERT
solution: DESERT — the rest are bodies of water.

question:
What is the longest common
one-syllable English word?
hint: Plural of what a bodybuilder has.
solution: STRENGTHS — 9 letters, one syllable.

question:
Unscramble:
NOITARTSIGER
solution: NOITARTSIGER reversed spells REGISTRATION.

question:
I contain "AND" but have nothing
to do with joining.
Water surrounds me. What am I?
hint: Land with water on every side.
solution: ISLAND — isl-AND-, surrounded by water.

question:
I start with E, end with E,
and contain only one letter.
What am I?
solution: ENVELOPE — starts and ends with E, holds one letter.

question:
Unscramble:
YTILIBISNOPSER
solution: YTILIBISNOPSER reversed spells RESPONSIBILITY.

question:
Find the metal hidden in:
"I sang old songs"
solution: sanG OLD — GOLD hides across 'sang old'.

question: Which word has five vowels in a row?
hint: Waiting in a line, as a verb.
solution: QUEUEING — u-e-u-e-i, five consecutive vowels.

question:
I am a word meaning "not allowed",
yet I carry a hidden "BID" inside.
What am I?
solution: FORBIDDEN — for-BID-den.

question:
Unscramble:
LATNEMNREVOG
hint: Related to those who rule a country.
solution: LATNEMNREVOG reversed spells GOVERNMENTAL.

question:
Which one is the odd one out?
SQUARE · TRIANGLE · SPHERE · RECTANGLE
solution: SPHERE is 3-D — the rest are 2-D shapes.

question:
Which 9-letter word is still a word
each time you remove a letter,
down to a single letter?
solution: STARTLING → STARTING → STARING → STRING → STING → SING → SIN → IN → I.

question:
Name a 7-letter word with no vowels
(a, e, i, o, u).
solution: RHYTHMS — seven letters, zero standard vowels.

question:
Unscramble:
NOITASILAICEPS
solution: NOITASILAICEPS reversed spells SPECIALISATION.

question:
What is the shortest English word
containing all five vowels?
hint: Six letters, means 'beautiful thinking'. Starts with EU.
solution: EUNOIA — 6 letters holding a, e, i, o, u.

question:
Unscramble:
MSILANRUOJ
solution: MSILANRUOJ reversed spells JOURNALISM.

question:
Which everyday word has all its letters
in reverse alphabetical order?
hint: Given everything without effort — like a baby with a spoon.
solution: SPOONFED — s, p, o, o, n, f, e, d descend alphabetically.

question:
Which 7-letter word for "needing a drink"
contains three consecutive letters
of the alphabet?
hint: How you feel after a long run.
solution: THIRSTY — contains R, S, T in a row.

question:
Which 15-letter word — the study of
fingerprints — repeats no letter?
solution: DERMATOGLYPHICS — 15 letters, all different.

question:
Find the planet hidden in:
"The calm arsenal stayed locked"
solution: calM ARSenal — MARS hides across 'calm arsenal'.

question:
Unscramble:
YHPARGOTOHP
solution: YHPARGOTOHP reversed spells PHOTOGRAPHY.

question:
What is the only number whose letters
are in alphabetical order?
solution: FORTY — f, o, r, t, y ascend alphabetically.

question:
What is the only number whose letters
are in reverse alphabetical order?
solution: ONE — o, n, e descend alphabetically.

question:
Which word is its own opposite,
meaning both "split apart"
and "stick together"?
hint: A butcher's knife is called a ___r.
solution: CLEAVE — a contronym meaning both to split and to cling.

question:
Unscramble:
NOITACINUMMOC
solution: NOITACINUMMOC reversed spells COMMUNICATION.

question:
Add one letter to EAR to get
what you feel when you are scared.
solution: FEAR — add F in front of EAR.

question:
What is the longest common word typed
using only the top row of a keyboard
(Q W E R T Y U I O P)?
solution: TYPEWRITER — every letter sits on the top row.

question:
Add a 2-letter prefix to VALUABLE and it
still means "priceless". Type the full word.
hint: The prefix is IN — but the meaning doesn't flip.
solution: INVALUABLE — 'in-' here intensifies instead of negating.

question:
Unscramble:
YTILANOSREP
solution: YTILANOSREP reversed spells PERSONALITY.

question:
Which 5-letter word about sound
contains four different vowels?
hint: The opposite of video, in a way.
solution: AUDIO — a, u, i, o plus a single D.

question:
What is the only common English word
ending in "-MT"?
hint: The past tense of what you do while asleep.
solution: DREAMT — the lone common '-mt' ending in English.

question:
Unscramble:
NOITANIGAMI
hint: Einstein said it is more important than knowledge.
solution: NOITANIGAMI reversed spells IMAGINATION.

question:
Which 7-letter word hides at least
seven smaller words inside it
without rearranging letters?
hint: A formal word meaning 'in that place'.
solution: THEREIN — the, there, he, her, here, ere, rein, in.

question:
FINAL BOSS:
I am a word of 3 letters.
Add 2 more and I become fewer.
What am I?
solution: FEW — add E and R and it becomes FEWER.
